commit.message:
fix: explicit agent-to-model mapping (no pattern matching)

Replace fragile pattern matching with explicit frozenset lists:
- CODEX_AGENT_IDS: 17 agents → gpt-5.2-codex (worker, brain, ft-*, ac-codex, etc.)
- REASON_AGENT_IDS: 10 agents → gpt-5.2 (product, rte, system-architect, etc.)
- Others → DEFAULT_MODEL (gpt-5-mini for fast/cheap tasks)

This guarantees PM/RTE use reasoning model and Dev/QA use code model,
regardless of role string content.

* chore: update check scripts to disable caching and enforce strict type checks

- Add strict graph-based typecheck (tsc -b) at the beginning of typecheck pipeline
- Disable turbo caching (--no-cache) for typecheck, lint, and build:types commands
- Ensures strict checks always run and catch migration-related type errors
- Prevents bypassing critical checks due to cache misses

This prevents issues like the Kysely migration type errors that were only caught
by the strict tsc -b check but missed by incremental turbo builds.

* chore: remove redundant build:types from check script

Since typecheck:graph runs 'tsc -b' which generates .d.ts files,
the separate build:types step is redundant and slows down the check.
Both achieve the same result: compiling packages and generating declarations.

This saves ~100ms on each check run.

even though the runtime supports it. By removing the explicit return type annotation,
TypeScript infers the type from the object literal, allowing the property.

This is safe because:
- The function still returns a valid config object
- Runtime behavior is unchanged
- Expo 55 runtime supports this property

fix: remove em dashes from install.ps1 - PS5.1 tokenizer bug

PS5.1's tokenizer miscounts bytes when a multi-byte UTF-8 character
(U+2014 em dash) appears in a double-quoted string. This corrupts the
parser state and causes every here-string after it to fail with 'An
empty pipe element is not allowed' errors (lines 260-281).

Three em dashes removed (lines 76, 208, 308). Replaced with hyphens.
PS5.1 parse error count: 37 -> 0.

fix: intelligent LLM routing + AC iteration improvements

- Add resolve_model_for_agent() for task-type based model routing:
  * Code agents (dev/worker/brain/infra/qa) → gpt-5.2-codex
  * Reasoning agents (architect/critic/reviewer) → gpt-5.2
  * Others → DEFAULT_MODEL (gpt-5-mini for fast tasks)
- Increase MAX_ADVERSARIAL_RETRIES from 2 to 3 (4 attempts total)
- Add retry_count: 3 on adversarial-review phase in feature-sprint
- Server startup now syncs agents with intelligent routing

Problem: All agents were using gpt-5-mini which produces lower quality
code, causing adversarial guard rejections (L1 hallucination, truncated
code, missing schemas).

Solution: Route code-producing agents to gpt-5.2-codex which has better
code generation capabilities. Combined with more retries, this allows
the AC iteration loop to improve output until quality threshold is met.

commit.message:
Use persistent RCON connection instead of connect-per-command (#26)

* Initial plan

* Add persistent RCON connection to container.Manager

- Add ServerManager interface to management package
- Create container package with RCONClient (Source RCON protocol)
- container.Manager uses persistent RCON connection:
  - Lazy-connect on first SendCommand
  - Reuse connection for subsequent calls
  - Auto-reconnect on broken pipe, EOF, or closed connection
  - Close() method tears down the connection
  - Thread-safe via mutex
- Comprehensive tests for RCON client and container Manager

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract maxRCONBodySize constant from magic number

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: auth deadline, retry error, concurrency docs, test safety

- rcon.go: Add 5s auth handshake deadline in Connect to prevent blocking
- rcon.go: Clarify RCON protocol type constant ambiguity with comment
- rcon.go: Fix concurrency doc (Connect/Close externally synchronized)
- rcon.go: Return empty string on response ID mismatch (Go convention)
- rcon.go: Improve comment on two-packet auth handling
- container.go: Fix retry logic to return retry error, not stale original
- container.go: Add compile-time ServerManager interface check
- container_test.go: Remove direct internal field access in tests
- container_test.go: Use real server shutdown for reconnect test
- rcon_test.go: Track and close active connections in fakeRCON.close()

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>
